title: Yui Ikari was it an accident she was in unit one
display_title: Yui Ikari was it an accident she was in unit one
wikitext:
[]: Yui Ikari was it an accident she was in unit one?             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Mar 26, 2005 9:54 am Continued from another thread. It is mentioned that Yui chose to stay in eva-one over death, but my question is “Did she get into the eva knowing she’d never leave it?” I tend to think not. My view of it was there was a mishap in the experiment and yui was faced with a choice stay with the eva or die. My reasons for this are simple, we see Yui portrayed as a caring woman with a lost of wisdom on top of her intelligence. If she knew what was going to happen and how horrible it would be why would she have brought her son to the lab that day and talk about showing him a bright future? Any fool can tell you that a child seeing something like that has got to be traumatic, and yui was no fool. Gendo seems shocked by what happens, and runs off to Seele to start the instrumentality project. If Yui knew what was going to happen why not tell her husband? Correct me if im wrong the same people that tells us she made the choice to stay in the eva, all use the word “accident” to describe what happened. If she got into the eva with no other goal then to join with it to help later, why would it be an accident?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2005 11:05 am I think you need to pay more attention to the Ashinoko flashback. Fuyutsuki : The same weather day after day... Fuyutsuki : The disappearance of autumn from this country is sad beyond words. Fuyutsuki : According to the scenario in Seele's secret Dead Sea Scrolls, Third Impact will definitely occur in a little over ten years. Yui : Seele and Gehirn - organizations for preventing the final tragedy. Fuyutsuki : I support your intentions, not Seele's. Yui : Fuyutsuki-sensei, it would be terribly dangerous to break that seal onto the world. Fuyutsuki : I've given all the materials to Ikari. It's not something one can do alone. Fuyutsuki : We won't repeat what happened last time. Fuyutsuki : Oh, and I've also been given a warning... Fuyutsuki : It seems that it would be no trouble at all for them to erase me. Yui : And those who survived as well. It's easy to exterminate people. Fuyutsuki : Still, that's no reason for you to become a test subject. Yui : Everything happens as it must. Yui : That's why I'm with Seele, for Shinji's sake... Fuyutsuki: Humans create Evangelion to copy God... Is this our true goal? Yui: Yes. Humans can only live on this planet, but Evangelion can live forever... together with the human soul that dwells within it. Even after 5 billion years, when the Earth, the Moon, and even the Sun have disappeared, it will still exist as long as even one person still lives. It will be very lonely, but as long as that one person still lives... Fuyutsuki: It will be the eternal proof that humankind has existed...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 26, 2005 1:19 pm Presuming that it wasn't an accident, and that it happened the way it ought to have, then there are a few concerns. The person who describes the event is Naoko Akagi, and is obvious in the show, she was out of the loop of a number of things, just as Misato is. So, then was the "salvage operation" purely for show? Did Gendo want to bring her back despite her wishes? Or was she not supposed to go in at that time, and it really was just an experiment?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Mar 26, 2005 1:36 pm Or the salvage operation wasn't designed to bring Yui back, but to bring something else?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 26, 2005 2:09 pm The character of Yui always strikes me as someone who is calm and always in control. Bad choice of word for what I'm trying to say here, but when you think back on all her comments, she is never in doubt. All her actions seem so deliberate. I think she knew what would happen, and chose to remain in the eva. Fuyusuki explicitly states, in EoE (Air), that remaining in Unit One was a conscious decision on her part. Fuyutsuki: Humans exist because they have the will to live... And that is the reason she remained within Eva. I don't think that remaining in Eva to be the last lonely traveler was her sole objective though. Her part to play with Shinji during third impact was arguably much more important. I'm also unsure as to whether she really was the person in the Eva as it floated into oblivion. But that's just me. Edit: Whoops, Forgot to mention one small, but important, point. Not only do I think Yui knew what she was doing when she went into the Eva. I think Gendou knew as well. In the scene where we see Shinji waving a Yui before the experiment, we first see Gendou with his glasses, and in his cold stare pose. (Though we did see this pose, without the glasses, in a preceding scene where Fuyutsuki confronts him over the coverup of second impact.) He also says not one word during this segment. I think he knew what was going to happen, and was complicit in Yui's plans, though he deeply regretted having to do so.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2005 4:27 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'm also unsure as to whether she really was the person in the Eva as it floated into oblivion. But that's just me.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 26, 2005 5:07 pm Reichu wrote: Then why did he enter a depression when the salvage operation failed? I think the idea is that Yui couldn't tell Gendo her true plans because he would never have let her carry them out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2005 5:44 pm Gendo being fully aware of Yui's intentions seems to go against everything, though. I'd disagree with this. I think Gendou was fully aware of Yui's intentions. From eps 24 Gendou: The final Angel will soon appear. When we erase that, our hopes will come true. Wait a little, Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 26, 2005 6:31 pm Reichu wrote: Then why did he enter a depression when the salvage operation failed?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 26, 2005 9:23 pm I just really feel that Gendou was fully aware of Yui's plans. It's either that or she decided to remain in eva without telling him anything. And that just doesn't seem likely. On the matter of Shinji. Yes, why would Yui leave her beloved son in the hands of a man like Gendou? I think that this was an overlooked part of their scenario. The explanation might be that Gendou simply went mad after Yui was gone, dispite being aware that she was leaving him. He shut himself off from the rest of the world and devoted himself to carrying out their agreement. I think Gendou's treatment of Shinji was pretty much explained in EoE during complementation. Yui stayed in the eva because she had a job to do. Synching with Shinji was part of it. Saving the whole human race from tangification was another. P.S Oh and on the who's in the eva question. I know. It IS Yui! Before I learned how to turn on subtitles in mplayer, I had to watch EoE while reading the script in front of me, so I missed the "Goodbye, Mother" line which proves the point beyond doubt. I had thought it was a fitting end for Gendou given his "So this is my Penance" line before he gets chomped, but I suppose he was just obliterated/banished to the 10th level of hell or whatever. I still wonder what exactly happened to him. I don't think he was just complemented, I always assummed he'd met with a far more grisly fate than anyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Mar 26, 2005 10:22 pm ObessiveMathsFreak wrote: I had thought it was a fitting end for Gendou given his "So this is my Penance" line before he gets chomped, but I suppose he was just obliterated/banished to the 10th level of hell or whatever. I still wonder what exactly happened to him. I don't think he was just complemented, I always assummed he'd met with a far more grisly fate than anyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Mar 26, 2005 10:36 pm The Ashinoko scene only mentions yui as a test subject, she does not say that she intends to merge with unit one. Like wise mention of her staying in the eva of her will does not mention if that choice was made knowing the only other option was death. Personaly I think if she'd intened to from the get go she'd have at least not had Shinji their to watch what happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 26, 2005 11:01 pm DatDude wrote: The Ashinoko scene only mentions yui as a test subject, she does not say that she intends to merge with unit one. Like wise mention of her staying in the eva of her will does not mention if that choice was made knowing the only other option was death. Personaly I think if she'd intened to from the get go she'd have at least not had Shinji their to watch what happened.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Mar 26, 2005 11:20 pm Bring the EVA online, other wise why try it again with unit two and asukas mom?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sun Mar 27, 2005 12:05 am The Eva Monkey wrote: The question is, if Yui always intended to be within the Eva, why is Gendo so hell bent on bringing her back against her intentions?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 27, 2005 12:40 am DatDude wrote: Bring the EVA online, other wise why try it again with unit two and asukas mom?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Mar 27, 2005 1:03 am Concidering the amout of times we hear the words " THIS SHOULDN'T BE POSSABLE "! in eva its its possable they didn't fully understand what they were copying and what would be needed to make it work. Yui's main characteristics have always been intellagence, wisdom and compasion. I don't see her having Shinji watch her accident/whatever just so he'd know deep down she was in the eva protecting him as somthing that would be in character for her. Though most of the time she's on screen its a flashback seen through the eyes of some one that in one way of another was in love with her, so out picture of her may be a bit tainted.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sun Mar 27, 2005 4:26 pm Reichu wrote: AA, I'm not convinced Rei was the intentional result of the Salvage -- or unintentional, at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sun Mar 27, 2005 4:54 pm DatDude wrote: I don't see her having Shinji watch her accident/whatever just so he'd know deep down she was in the eva protecting him as somthing that would be in character for her.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 27, 2005 5:13 pm AchtungAffen wrote: Don't you think there's a relation between the salvage process and Rei's creation?
